{"step_by_step_thinking": "Prosopagnosia is defined as the inability to recognize known and new faces, also known as facial or visual agnosia. This condition arises from damage to the fusiform face area in the inferior temporal cortex. The term 'prosopagnosia' comes from Greek, meaning 'face recognition deficit'. The documents clearly state that prosopagnosia is a visual recognition disorder, not an auditory one. Auditory recognition deficits are described separately in the context of auditory agnosia, such as phonagnosia (inability to recognize familiar voices) or nonverbal auditory agnosia (inability to comprehend nonverbal sounds). There is no mention in any of the documents that prosopagnosia is equivalent to or known as a lack of auditory recognition. Therefore, the claim that prosopagnosia is also known as lack of auditory recognition is incorrect.", "answer_choice": "B"}
